nodes.name: eBay Guaranteed Fit
nodes.desc: Fitment guarantee for auto parts ensuring compatibility with buyer's vehicle. US program launched October 2025 as 'Fitment Plus Auto'.
nodes.shouldHaveNameRationale: Pass - user directly selects, enrolls in, purchases, toggles, or visibly interacts with this feature, product, or trust signal
nodes.recommendedNamingTreatment: Consolidate into parent trust brand
nodes.evidenceNotes: Reclassified from platform_trust to item_trust. Verify Guaranteed Fit badge appears at listing level on individual auto parts items.
nodes.why_fix_is_right: Guaranteed Fit badge/promise appears on specific auto parts listings. It is an item-level fitment compatibility promise — the badge attaches to individual listings, qualifying it as item_trust by the Tier C boundary rule.
nodes.rationale: Per boundary rule: 'if the resulting badge or promise attaches to listings/items, classify as item_trust.' The Guaranteed Fit badge appears on specific auto parts listings asserting compatibility with a buyer's vehicle — an item-level accuracy assurance.
nodes.slide_basis: Core definitions — item_trust boundary rule: badge/promise attaches to listings/items
nodes.context_note: Ambiguous: prior audit classified as platform_trust because eBay backs the guarantee broadly (similar to EMBG). Reclassified to item_trust because the badge appears on specific auto parts listings and the promise is about item-level fitment compatibility. Tier C: program-level assurance, not item-by-item inspection.

nodes.evidenceNotes: Reclassified from platform_trust to item_trust. Verify Guaranteed Fit badge appears at listing level on individual auto parts items.
nodes.why_fix_is_right: Guaranteed Fit (duplicate/variant) — same item-level fitment promise as ebay-guaranteed-fit. Badge attaches to specific auto parts listings.
nodes.rationale: Per boundary rule: 'if the resulting badge or promise attaches to listings/items, classify as item_trust.' The Guaranteed Fit badge appears on specific auto parts listings asserting compatibility with a buyer's vehicle — an item-level accuracy assurance.
nodes.slide_basis: Core definitions — item_trust boundary rule: badge/promise attaches to listings/items
nodes.context_note: Duplicate of ebay-guaranteed-fit. Reclassified consistently. Both should consolidate.

nodes.name: Certified Refurbished
nodes.desc: Top-tier eBay Refurbished condition with manufacturer certification and comprehensive warranty.
nodes.shouldHaveNameRationale: Pass - user directly selects, enrolls in, purchases, toggles, or visibly interacts with this feature, product, or trust signal
nodes.recommendedNamingTreatment: Use as label - functional descriptor, no brand investment needed
nodes.evidenceNotes: Marketed as owned name but functions as a label; recommend downgrade. Source: https://www.ebay.com/help/selling/listings/creating-managing-listings/item-condi
nodes.why_fix_is_right: Certified Refurbished is the top condition tier of eBay Refurbished — manufacturer-certified condition with a comprehensive warranty on the specific listing. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 20 places eBay Refurbished under Item Trust Tier C. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 9 accuracy: manufacturer certification of a specific item's condition is item trust by definition.
nodes.rationale: Item trust Tier C sub-tier. Manufacturer certification attaches to the specific item. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 4, 9, 13, 20.
nodes.context_note: Top condition tier within eBay Refurbished program. Same trust layer as the parent program.

nodes.why_fix_is_right: Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 4 defines seller trust as "account-level indicators that help buyers assess a seller's reliability and service quality." nodes.rationale: Seller-account defect metric. Measures seller-specific service quality. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 4.
nodes.context_note: Primary input to TRS eligibility. Seller trust layer.

nodes.shouldHaveNameRationale: Pass - user directly selects, enrolls in, purchases, toggles, or visibly interacts with this feature, product, or trust signal
nodes.recommendedNamingTreatment: Actively govern - trust-signal tier, naming standards apply
nodes.why_fix_is_right: TCGplayer authenticates and grades individual trading cards — the grade attaches to each specific card, not to the seller account or the platform broadly. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 13 Tier A: "eBay intermediates or item is brand-direct; physically authenticated by a trusted 3rd party partner." TCGplayer Authentication Center is exactly this. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 14, 16 confirm trading card grading as Tier A item trust.
nodes.rationale: Item-level authentication and grading by a trusted 3rd party partner. Tier A: physically authenticated per Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 13.
nodes.context_note: Part of eBay AG ecosystem for trading cards. Tier A item trust.

nodes.name: VeRO Program
nodes.desc: Verified Rights Owner program allowing trademark and copyright holders to report intellectual property infringement.
nodes.shouldHaveNameRationale: Pass - user directly selects, enrolls in, purchases, toggles, or visibly interacts with this feature, product, or trust signal
nodes.recommendedNamingTreatment: Actively govern - trust-signal tier, naming standards apply
nodes.why_fix_is_right: VeRO is an IP protection program operated at the marketplace level, allowing rights holders to report violations across all listings globally. It is not an account-level indicator of seller quality. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 4 defines platform trust as marketplace-wide integrity measures. VeRO is eBay's mechanism for IP enforcement across the entire platform — a classic integrity measure.
nodes.rationale: Platform-wide IP protection and enforcement program. Marketplace integrity measure. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 4.
nodes.context_note: Affects sellers via listing removal, but the program itself is a platform-level IP governance tool, not a seller reputation signal.

nodes.why_fix_is_right: SGS performs a 150-point physical inspection of each individual vehicle. nodes.rationale: Item-level physical inspection by a trusted 3rd party partner. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 4, 13.

nodes.why_fix_is_right: Detailed Seller Ratings (DSRs) is a buyer-facing 5-star rating system where buyers rate sellers on communication, shipping speed, shipping costs, and item description accuracy — directly helping buyers assess specific sellers.
nodes.rationale: Per seller_trust definition: 'account-level indicators that help buyers assess a specific seller's reliability, quality, performance, identity, or qualifications.' DSRs and performance tiers are canonical seller quality signals.
nodes.slide_basis: Core definitions — seller_trust: account-level indicators of seller quality
nodes.context_note: Currently classified as non_trust. DSRs are being phased out in some markets but remain buyer-visible and are a canonical seller reputation signal. Consistent with Seller Performance Standards (seller_trust).

nodes.name: Vehicle History Report
nodes.desc: Free AutoCheck vehicle history report on Motors listings showing accidents, title issues, odometer fraud, ownership count. Trust signal for high-value purchases.
nodes.shouldHaveNameRationale: Pass - user directly selects, enrolls in, purchases, toggles, or visibly interacts with this feature, product, or trust signal
nodes.recommendedNamingTreatment: Owner review required - determine naming governance and accountability
nodes.why_fix_is_right: The AutoCheck vehicle history report is tied to a specific VIN (vehicle). It provides item-level accuracy information: accidents, title issues, odometer fraud, ownership count. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 9 defines accuracy as "Does the item match my expectations?" — vehicle history answers that question for the specific vehicle listed. The object of the promise is the vehicle item, not the marketplace broadly.
nodes.rationale: Item-level accuracy signal for a specific vehicle (VIN-tied). Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 4, 9.
nodes.context_note: Ambiguous: eBay provides the report as a platform service, but the content is item-specific. Primary trust object is the vehicle — classify as item_trust accuracy.

nodes.why_fix_is_right: Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 4 defines platform trust as "marketplace-wide assurances and integrity measures." A 2011 marketing campaign with the CFDA provides no ongoing buyer assurance, no resolution mechanism, and no verifiable promise — it cannot be invoked by a buyer or seller in a transaction. It is brand awareness and commercial language, which the task framework classifies as not trust-related.
nodes.rationale: Marketing campaign, not a trust mechanism. No assurance, resolution, or vetting function. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 4.
nodes.context_note: Campaign language only. No ongoing buyer protection or assurance function after 2011.

nodes.shouldHaveNameRationale: Pass - user directly selects, enrolls in, purchases, toggles, or visibly interacts with this feature, product, or trust signal
nodes.recommendedNamingTreatment: Actively govern - naming standards, brand voice, and governance docs required
nodes.why_fix_is_right: Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 14 lists "In the PSA Vault" as a Tier A item trust signal for collectibles. eBay Vault provides equivalent authenticated secure storage — items must be authenticated before admission, and storage in a verified vault backs the item's authenticity and condition claim. The promise attaches to the specific item held in vault.
nodes.rationale: Item trust Tier A. Authenticated vault storage backing item-level authenticity and condition promise. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 4, 13, 14.
nodes.context_note: Tier A per PSA Vault precedent on slide 14. If eBay Vault does not require prior physical authentication, tier should be reviewed and may shift to Tier C.

nodes.why_fix_is_right: Beckett Grading Services authenticates and grades individual trading cards and collectibles. nodes.rationale: Item-level 3rd party physical grading and authentication. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 4, 13, 16.

nodes.name: Certificate of Authenticity
nodes.desc: Third-party authentication documentation (PSA/DNA, JSA, Beckett) required for autographed sports memorabilia $250+ to combat forgeries.
nodes.shouldHaveNameRationale: Pass - user directly selects, enrolls in, purchases, toggles, or visibly interacts with this feature, product, or trust signal
nodes.recommendedNamingTreatment: Owner review required - determine naming governance and accountability
nodes.why_fix_is_right: A COA (PSA/DNA, JSA, Beckett) is a document attached to a specific autographed memorabilia item verifying that item's authenticity. It is item-specific, not a platform-wide assurance. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 13 Tier A applies: physically authenticated by a trusted 3rd party partner. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 9 defines authenticity as "Is the item real, and who is accountable?" — a COA answers exactly that question for the specific item.
nodes.rationale: Item-level 3rd party authentication document for a specific autographed item. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 4, 9, 13.
nodes.context_note: Required for autographed sports memorabilia $250+. Attaches to the individual item, not the seller account or platform broadly.

The authentication applied to the specific item. Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 13 Tier A: physically authenticated by an eBay authenticator or trusted 3rd party. This program did exactly that — even though it is now deprecated.
nodes.rationale: Historical item-level authentication service. Tier A per Per the McKinsey Item Trust Definition Sprint (January 2026), slide 13. Replaced by Authenticity Guarantee in 2020. Classification is correct for historical accuracy.
nodes.context_note: Deprecated (replaced by AG in 2020). Reclassified to item_trust for historical accuracy; recommend excluding from active trust taxonomy.
